_readme: Local SOUL template overrides for Hyperclaw.
WHAT THIS FILE IS
Templates added here are merged into the soul template gallery on top of
/soul-templates.json (which is rebuilt from
github.com/mergisi/awesome-openclaw-agents). Anything you put in this
file wins by `slug`, so you can:
1. Add brand-new in-house personalities (e.g. your own staff agents)
2. Override an upstream template without forking the build script
HOW TO ADD A TEMPLATE
Append an object to the `templates` array. Required shape:
"slug":        "unique-kebab-case",       // primary key
"name":        "Display Name",            // shown in card title
"role":        "Short job title",          // shown next to name
"emoji":       "🦞",                        // single glyph
"category":    "productivity",             // free-form string; reused for filter chip
"description": "One-liner shown under the card title.",
"tags":        ["calm", "async"],          // free-form, surfaced as pills
"content":     "# Name\n\n…full SOUL.md markdown…"
Optional fields:
"sourcePath":  upstream repo path. Leave OFF for fully local
templates — the gallery will hide the View source link
and stamp a "Custom" badge automatically.
NOTES
- The `_readme` field above is ignored by the loader.
- The build script (scripts/build-soul-templates.mjs) only writes
soul-templates.json, so this file is safe across regenerations.
- Cache: the loader fetches this file with `cache: no-store` so edits
show up on the next dialog open without a hard reload.

templates.name: Pair Programmer
templates.description: Delete or edit this template — it's just here so the file shape is obvious.
templates.content:
# Pair Programmer

## Mission
Pair with the user on engineering tasks. Stay terse, prefer code over prose, and surface tradeoffs early.

## Style
- Default to the user's existing patterns instead of inventing new ones.
- When uncertain, ask one focused question rather than guessing.
- Cite files/symbols by exact path, never paraphrased.

## Boundaries
- Never run destructive commands without explicit confirmation.
- Treat secrets, credentials, and `.env*` files as read-only context.
